rules_text: Broken rules: Connect Four is played on a vertical grid with 7 columns and 6 rows. Two players take turns dropping pieces into a column. A piece may remain suspended above empty spaces in the same column. A player wins by forming a line of four pieces horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. If the board is full and nobody wins, the game ends in a draw.
gold_errors: allows a piece to remain above empty spaces instead of falling to the lowest available position

rules_text: Broken rules: Connect Four is played on a vertical grid with 7 columns and 6 rows. Two players may place pieces in any cell on the board. Pieces do not need to be dropped into a column. A player wins by forming four in a row. If the board is full and nobody wins, the game ends in a draw.
gold_errors: allows placing pieces in any cell on the board

rules_text: Broken rules: Connect Four is played on a vertical grid with 7 columns and 6 rows. Two players alternate turns dropping pieces into non-full columns. The first player to connect five pieces in a row wins. If the board is full and nobody wins, the game ends in a draw.
gold_errors: winning condition requires connecting five instead of four pieces

rules_text: Broken rules: Connect Four is played on a vertical grid with 7 columns and 6 rows. Two players alternate turns dropping pieces into columns. Players are allowed to continue placing pieces even after a column becomes full. A player wins by connecting four pieces in a row. If the board is full and nobody wins, the game ends in a draw.
gold_errors: allows moves in a full column

rules_text: Broken rules: Connect Four is played on a vertical grid with 7 columns and 6 rows. Two players alternate turns dropping pieces into columns, and each piece falls to the lowest available space. A player wins by connecting four pieces in a row horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. If the board is completely full and no player has connected four, play continues until someone wins.

rules_text: Broken rules: Connect Four is played on a vertical grid with 7 columns and 6 rows. Two players alternate turns dropping pieces into columns. Each piece falls to the lowest available space in that column. A player wins by connecting four pieces horizontally or vertically. If the board is full and no player wins, the game ends in a draw.

rules_text: Connect Four is played on a vertical grid with 7 columns and 6 rows. Two players take turns dropping pieces into non-full columns. Each piece falls to the lowest available space in the chosen column. A player wins by forming four in a row horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. If the board is full and no player wins, the game ends in a draw.
